name: Meta (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the savannahs and gallery forests of the Llanos Orientales
the rivers that crisscross the eastern plains

description: A green field crossed by eight thin white horizontal lines spanning the flag from hoist to fly.
history: Meta Department was created in 1959 from the former Meta Intendancy. The department lies in the Llanos Orientales (eastern plains) east of the Andes and is named after the Meta River, the principal tributary of the Orinoco in Colombia.

features.symbolism: the green field representing the savannahs and gallery forests of the Llanos Orientales
eight thin white horizontal lines across the green field, evoking the rivers that crisscross the eastern plains
